action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 9 achieved a new best balanced_accuracy of 0.8514 (up from 0.7275) using natural-sampled standard CE training with layer-wise LR decay and drop_path=0.1 for 20 epochs, followed by post-hoc logit adjustment (tau=1.5). The training curves show severe overfitting (train_acc ~99% vs val_acc ~77%), which means the classifier head is heavily biased toward head classes despite good learned features. Analysis: Decoupled training (classifier retraining, cRT) is a proven long-tailed learning strategy: freeze the well-learned backbone and retrain only the classifier head with balanced sampling. This directly removes head-class bias without destroying the representation. It is also extremely fast (~30-60s) since only ~77K head parameters are updated. Decision: Implement classifier retraining (cRT) on the existing best.pt (iter 9) for 10 epochs using WeightedRandomSampler and head-only AdamW. After retraining, quickly tune tau on the new checkpoint and submit eval. I will also smoke-test predict.py locally before submitting.
action.command: python retrain_head.py && python tune_tau_crt.py && mkdir -p /tmp/fake_test && cp /data/val.pt /tmp/fake_test/test.pt && python predict.py --data_path /tmp/fake_test --checkpoint checkpoints/crt_adj.pt --output /tmp/fake_test/out.json && echo 'Smoke test OK' && cat /tmp/fake_test/out.json | head -c 200
